sources.label: Linear redesign hierarchy study
borrowed: Quiet alignment-first hierarchy for dense operational navigation
Compressed secondary metadata instead of ornamental dashboard filler
rejected: Issue-tracker chrome density that would overpower booking truth

sources.label: Vercel dashboard navigation rollout
borrowed: Persistent sidebar structure with unified navigation order
Collapsible secondary navigation instead of reloading whole shells
rejected: Consumer-style marketing framing in an operational workspace

sources.label: Vercel dashboard overview
borrowed: Scope awareness in the header and consistent route-level chrome
Search and navigation remaining separate from the active work surface
rejected: Project dashboard card density that would blur the booking narrative

sources.label: Carbon data table usage
borrowed: Compact scan-first row structure for the exception queue
Contextual use of toolbar-like actions near dense data
rejected: Turning the booking panel into a full data table

sources.label: Carbon status indicators
borrowed: Tight status chips with clear severity meanings
Left-aligned status language for fast scanning
rejected: Overuse of brightly differentiated status colors

sources.label: Carbon disabled states
borrowed: Separate blocked versus hidden versus readable-but-not-actionable treatment
rejected: Hiding relevant recovery state when staff still need to read it

sources.label: Carbon read-only states
borrowed: Keep information readable while removing interactivity
Read-only controls stay reviewable by keyboard and screen reader
rejected: Switching disabled content into read-only styling when it should stay blocked

sources.label: Carbon dialog pattern
borrowed: Reserve modal interruption only for real confirmation moments
rejected: Modal treatment for ordinary stale-owner or reminder repair states

sources.label: NHS buttons guidance
borrowed: One clear primary action at a time
Verb-first action labels
rejected: Multiple same-weight primary buttons per panel

sources.label: NHS summary list guidance
borrowed: Definition-list summaries for patient preference and state rows
rejected: A long check-answers review-page treatment

sources.label: NHS table guidance
borrowed: Captioned tabular comparison thinking for the exception queue and compact metrics
rejected: Responsive table conversion for the main compare plane

sources.label: NHS content guide
borrowed: Factual and unambiguous service wording
Short sentences for blocked and pending posture
rejected: Soft reassurance that would hide pending or blocked truth

sources.label: WAI dialog modal pattern
borrowed: Keep modal behavior tightly scoped to true dialogs
rejected: Modalizing the recovery panel

sources.label: WAI keyboard interface guidance
borrowed: Predictable sequential focus order inside the workspace shell
rejected: Roving keyboard models where simple tab order is clearer

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 focus appearance
borrowed: Strong visible focus outlines across queue, slot, and recovery controls
rejected: Subtle low-contrast focus treatments

sources.label: WCAG 2.2 focus order
borrowed: Keyboard order that preserves meaning even when the visual layout uses multiple rails
rejected: Visual-column order that would confuse the booking workflow

sources.label: WAI status message technique
borrowed: Use of role=status for booking and slot-state updates
rejected: Moving focus just to announce pending or recovery status

sources.label: WCAG reflow understanding
borrowed: Single-stack mobile layout without two-dimensional scrolling
rejected: Desktop-only multi-rail assumptions

sources.label: Playwright browser contexts
borrowed: Separate writer and reader contexts for multi-user shell proof
rejected: Reusing one context for conflicting ownership postures

sources.label: Playwright aria snapshots
borrowed: Accessibility tree captures for compare-live and pending-confirmation states
rejected: Relying only on screenshots for accessibility proof

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Automated accessibility checks treated as one layer of proof, not the whole story
rejected: Treating automated checks as complete accessibility sign-off

sources.label: Playwright trace viewer
borrowed: Trace capture for multi-user and recovery scenarios
rejected: Debugging route drift only from console output
